overview: Create an installable OpenCode plugin that starts a localhost sidecar HTTP server exposing a minimal OpenAI-compatible API backed by a real OpenCode instance, without modifying OpenCode's built-in router.
goals: Install as a normal OpenCode plugin.
Auto-start a local sidecar server when the plugin initializes.
Expose only currently connected OpenCode providers and models.
Return model IDs in provider/model form.
Map upstream responses and errors into stable OpenAI-compatible JSON.
Prove the full flow works against a real OpenCode instance, not only mocks.
nonGoals: Extending the native opencode serve HTTP router.
Supporting streaming responses in the MVP.
Supporting embeddings, images, audio, Assistants, Responses API, or tool-calling passthrough in the MVP.
Adding a browser UI or dashboard.
successMetrics: A configured OpenCode environment starts the plugin and exposes a healthy localhost sidecar within 5 seconds.
A real end-to-end verification run shows GET /v1/models returning only connected models and no disconnected models.
A valid non-streaming POST /v1/chat/completions request against a live OpenCode instance returns OpenAI-compatible assistant text.
Known invalid requests such as stream=true, empty messages, and unknown models return stable 400 errors, while upstream outages return stable 502 errors.
openQuestions: What exact manifest or package metadata does the current OpenCode plugin loader require for plugin installation?
Should GET /health perform a live upstream probe on every request or report last-known reachability within the cache window?
Which upstream fields, if any, are stable enough to map into OpenAI usage tokens in the MVP?

stack.framework: OpenCode plugin with a Bun HTTP sidecar server
stack.hosting: Local sidecar process started from the plugin and bound to localhost
stack.auth: Optional bearer token for sidecar clients and optional upstream basic auth credentials for talking to OpenCode
stack.testing: Bun test for unit and integration coverage plus a real OpenCode end-to-end verification script

routes.name: Health
routes.purpose: Return sidecar readiness and upstream reachability summary for local startup verification.

routes.name: List Models
routes.purpose: Expose only currently connected OpenCode models as OpenAI-compatible model entries.

routes.name: Chat Completions
routes.purpose: Accept non-streaming OpenAI-style chat completion requests and return mapped assistant text.

uiNotes: No browser UI is in scope; the deliverable is an HTTP JSON API only.
The default bind address must be 127.0.0.1 and the default port must be 4097, with both remaining configurable.
If apiKey is configured, routes require a bearer token and failures use a consistent OpenAI-like error envelope.
Health and error responses must avoid leaking upstream credentials, prompts, completions, or disconnected model metadata.

importFormat.description: Inbound client payloads follow the OpenAI-compatible /v1/chat/completions JSON request shape; there is no separate file import in the MVP.

rules: The plugin must use supported OpenCode plugin lifecycle behavior and must not inject routes into the built-in server.
The sidecar binds to 127.0.0.1 by default and the port defaults to 4097 unless overridden by config.
Only currently connected providers are exposed, and each model ID must use the exact provider/model format.
Disconnected providers and providers with no models must not appear in GET /v1/models.
The sidecar must not invent fake models or stale availability data in the MVP.
POST /v1/chat/completions requires model and a non-empty messages array, and stream=true is rejected in the MVP.
Model resolution must verify both provider and model against the current connected model catalog.
Messages are serialized into a stable text prompt with an optional System section followed by a chronological transcript that preserves the final user turn at the end.
Each chat request creates a fresh OpenCode session and sends exactly one message; session reuse is out of scope for the MVP.
Assistant response mapping concatenates text parts in order, ignores non-text parts in the MVP, and returns OpenAI-style chat.completion JSON.
Upstream unreachable, auth, or execution failures return sanitized 502 responses, while client validation failures return sanitized 400 responses.
Prompts, completions, tokens, and credentials must never be logged by default.
defaultAgent defaults to build when the upstream flow requires an agent and no override is provided.

stories.title: Scaffold the Bun TypeScript OpenCode plugin project
stories.description: As a maintainer, I want a clean plugin scaffold with scripts and package installs so that later stories can add behavior without guessing the project shape.
acceptanceCriteria: Initialize a Bun and TypeScript project with the approved source layout, a plugin entrypoint, and scripts for build, test, typecheck, and test:e2e:real.
Add the baseline development installs with `bun add -d typescript @types/bun` before feature code is introduced.

Repository setup declares the plugin packaging metadata required for OpenCode installation and the default configuration keys enabled, host, port, apiKey, upstreamBaseUrl, upstreamUsername, upstreamPassword, defaultAgent, requestTimeoutMs, and modelsCacheTtlMs.
Example: running `bun run build` from a clean checkout produces the plugin build output without any manual file creation.
Negative case: if the plugin entrypoint or packaging metadata is missing, build or typecheck fails with a clear actionable error instead of producing a partial plugin.

stories.title: Parse config and start the sidecar once per process
stories.description: As an OpenCode user, I want plugin startup to parse config safely and launch the sidecar only once so that installation is reliable and repeatable.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement config parsing with defaults for enabled=true, host=127.0.0.1, port=4097, defaultAgent=build, and sensible timeout and cache defaults.
Implement the plugin entry so initialization is idempotent and never binds multiple sidecar listeners in the same process.
Startup and failure logging includes plugin name, bind host, bind port, and sanitized upstream target information when available.
Example: if initialization runs twice in one process, the second call reuses the existing sidecar instance and logs that startup was already completed.
Negative case: invalid numeric config such as a non-numeric port or disallowed host value causes startup to fail clearly and leaves no half-started listener behind.

stories.title: Implement the OpenCode upstream client
stories.description: As the sidecar, I want a typed upstream client for provider and session APIs so that compatibility routes can depend on one normalized integration layer.
acceptanceCriteria: Create an upstream client that wraps GET /provider, POST /session, and POST /session/:id/message with shared timeouts and optional basic auth support.
Normalize upstream success and error payloads into internal types that route handlers can consume without duplicating parsing logic.
Implement short-lived model discovery caching that respects modelsCacheTtlMs and refreshes after expiry.
Example: with a reachable upstream and valid credentials, the client returns normalized provider, session, and assistant message data to callers.
Negative case: upstream timeout, network failure, or auth failure is converted into a sanitized internal error that does not leak credentials or raw upstream stack traces.

stories.title: Stand up the sidecar server and health route
stories.description: As a local client, I want a stable sidecar server with health checks and auth middleware so that I can verify readiness before using compatibility endpoints.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement the Bun HTTP server with shared JSON helpers, request parsing, consistent OpenAI-like error envelopes, and optional bearer token middleware when apiKey is configured.
Add GET /health that reports sidecar health and upstream reachability when feasible, plus plugin name or version when available.
Request logging records method, route, status, and failure reason without logging prompts, completions, tokens, or credentials.
Example: GET /health on the default configuration returns a 200 JSON response showing the sidecar is healthy on 127.0.0.1:4097.
Negative case: when apiKey is configured and the client omits or sends the wrong bearer token, the route returns a 401 OpenAI-like error response and no handler logic executes.

stories.title: Expose connected models at GET /v1/models
stories.description: As an OpenAI-compatible client, I want to list only the models OpenCode can use right now so that I can select a valid model without guesswork.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement a models mapper that filters to connected providers only, flattens provider model maps, and emits OpenAI-style model entries with ids in provider/model form.
GET /v1/models returns a stable JSON payload with a data array and excludes disconnected providers and providers that currently expose zero models.
The route uses the cached provider catalog when it is fresh and refreshes from upstream when the cache is missing or expired.
Example: if only provider `openai` is connected and it exposes model `gpt-5.1`, the response contains `openai/gpt-5.1` and no entries from disconnected providers.
Negative case: malformed provider metadata or an upstream failure returns a sanitized 502 error envelope instead of partial, stale, or invented model data.

stories.title: Validate and serialize chat completion requests
stories.description: As a compatibility client, I want request validation and stable prompt serialization so that unsupported OpenAI fields fail clearly and valid requests map predictably into OpenCode.
acceptanceCriteria: Validate POST /v1/chat/completions input so model is required, messages is a non-empty array, and stream is either false or omitted.
Resolve the requested provider and model against the current connected model catalog before any upstream session is created.
Serialize messages into a deterministic prompt format with a System section when present and a chronological transcript that preserves the final user turn at the end.
Example: system, user, assistant, and user messages are converted into one stable text prompt that keeps the last user instruction as the final section.
Negative case: stream=true, empty messages, unknown provider/model, or multimodal content arrays return a 400 OpenAI-like invalid request response naming the bad field.

stories.title: Execute non-streaming chat completions and map responses
stories.description: As a compatibility client, I want successful non-streaming chat completions to return OpenAI-style assistant text so that existing tools can consume the sidecar with minimal changes.
acceptanceCriteria: For each valid request, create a fresh OpenCode session, send one message using the resolved model and configured agent, and collect assistant response parts.
Map successful upstream output into OpenAI-style chat.completion JSON with id, object, created, model, choices[0].index, choices[0].message.role, choices[0].message.content, and finish_reason.
Include usage only when the upstream response provides fields that can be mapped cleanly; otherwise omit usage from the success response.
Example: assistant text parts `Hello` and ` world` are concatenated into `Hello world` in choices[0].message.content.
Negative case: upstream execution failure, missing assistant content, or a response with no text parts returns a sanitized 502 error instead of malformed success JSON.

stories.title: Add automated unit and integration coverage
stories.description: As a maintainer, I want automated coverage around mapping, validation, and route behavior so that compatibility regressions are caught before real OpenCode verification.
acceptanceCriteria: Add unit tests for config parsing, provider filtering, model mapping, request validation, prompt serialization, response mapping, and error mapping.
Add integration tests with a mocked upstream for GET /health, GET /v1/models, and POST /v1/chat/completions.
Example: a mocked provider response with one connected provider and one disconnected provider yields only the connected provider's models in GET /v1/models.
Negative case: invalid JSON, unsupported streaming, unknown model, and upstream timeout produce the expected 400 or 502 error shapes without leaking credentials.
bun test, bun run typecheck, and bun run build all pass before the project is considered ready for real end-to-end verification.

stories.title: Verify the plugin against a real OpenCode instance
stories.description: As a maintainer, I want a documented real OpenCode verification flow so that MVP completion is proven against live upstream behavior rather than mocks alone.
acceptanceCriteria: Provide a documented manual flow or helper script exposed as `bun run test:e2e:real` that installs or connects the plugin to a real OpenCode instance, waits for GET /health, calls GET /v1/models, sends POST /v1/chat/completions, and verifies the returned shape.
Document prerequisites, required OpenCode setup, example curl commands, and expected outputs in the repository README or dedicated testing documentation.
Example: selecting a model returned by GET /v1/models and sending a real non-streaming chat request returns OpenAI-compatible assistant text from a live OpenCode instance.
Negative case: no connected providers or unreachable upstream produces a readable failure response and confirms that no fake models are advertised.
The MVP is not complete until `bun run test:e2e:real` succeeds against a real OpenCode instance.
